tagline: Capture, organize, and tackle your to-dos from anywhere.
subheadline: Escape the clutter and chaos—unleash your productivity with Trello.
value_proposition: A visual task and project management tool that helps individuals and teams capture, organize, and act on work from any device.
target_audience: Teams of 2–2,000 across marketing, engineering, design, product, and remote work; also individuals seeking personal productivity

testimonials: Great for simplifying complex processes. As a manager, I can chunk [processes] down into bite-sized pieces for my team and then delegate that out, but still keep a bird's-eye view. — Joey Rosenberg, Global Leadership Director at Women Who Code
Whether someone is in the office, working from home, or working on-site with a client, everyone can share context and information through Trello. — Sumeet Moghe, Product Manager at ThoughtWorks

We used Trello to provide clarity on steps, requirements, and procedures. This was exceptional when communicating with teams that had deep cultural and language differences. — Jefferson Scomacao, Development Manager at IKEA/PTC
metrics: 75% of organizations report that Trello delivers value to their business within 30 days (TechValidate Survey)
81% of customers chose Trello for its ease of use (TechValidate Survey)
74% of customers say Trello has improved communication with their co-workers and teams (TechValidate Survey)

pricing.price_anchoring: Free plan prominently featured as default entry point; paid tiers positioned by team size and feature depth (Standard for scaling teams, Premium for up to 100 users with multiple views, Enterprise for large org administration)

features: Inbox: Capture to-dos from email (forward to Trello), Slack, and Microsoft Teams with AI-generated summaries
Boards: Visual kanban-style task tracking from to-do to done
Planner: Calendar sync with drag-and-drop time allocation for focused work
Automation: No-code built-in automation on every board
Card Mirroring: View to-dos from multiple boards in one place
differentiators: AI features now available for all Premium and Enterprise tiers
No-code automation is built into every Trello board — no add-on required
Email-to-Trello: forward emails directly to Inbox; AI transforms them into organized to-dos
Native integrations with Slack and Microsoft Teams that push messages directly into Trello Inbox
Card mirroring allows to-dos to appear across multiple boards simultaneously
trust_signals: TechValidate third-party survey data cited for all key metrics
Testimonials attributed to named individuals with titles and organizations
Atlassian Privacy Policy referenced at signup
GDPR/privacy compliance language in cookie consent flow

design_patterns.layout: Top nav → hero with email signup → feature showcase (Inbox/Boards/Planner) → use-case section → integrations/automation highlight → social proof carousel → pricing tier overview → final CTA
design_patterns.color_scheme: Trello blue as primary brand color; white backgrounds with clean card-based sections
design_patterns.typography: Sans-serif, clean and modern; headline-heavy with short punchy copy
design_patterns.visual_style: Friendly and minimal; approachable rather than technical
notable_patterns: Inline email signup in hero (no modal)
Feature tabs (Inbox/Boards/Planner) with likely animated product screenshots
Rotating testimonial carousel with TechValidate stat interleaved
Use-case cards for team verticals (marketing, engineering, design, etc.)
Video CTA in hero ('Watch video') as soft secondary engagement

emotional_tone: Friendly and aspirational with a productivity empowerment angle; accessible rather than enterprise-serious
key_objections_addressed: Too complex for small teams: addressed with 'It's simple — sign-up, create a board, and you're off!'
Cost concerns: Free plan prominently featured throughout
Works for my team size: '...whether you're a team of 2 or 2,000'
Remote/distributed team friction: dedicated remote team use case and testimonial from ThoughtWorks
Cross-cultural/language barriers: IKEA/PTC testimonial directly addresses this
Time-to-value: '75% of organizations report value within 30 days' stat
missing_or_weak: No named press or media logos (TechCrunch, Forbes, G2, etc.) — social proof relies entirely on TechValidate surveys and three testimonials
No live user count or growth signal (e.g. '10 million teams use Trello')
No security/compliance badges visible (SOC 2, GDPR, ISO) despite enterprise tier offering
Pricing page not shown inline — users must navigate away to see actual prices, adding friction
AI features announced via banner but not deeply explained or positioned as a differentiator on the main page
No comparison table vs. competitors (Asana, Monday, Notion, ClickUp) — differentiation is implicit not explicit
Video CTA present but no thumbnail/preview to incentivize clicks
Feature depth for power users (automations, views, APIs) undersold on this page
standout_elements: TechValidate survey stats are unusually specific and credible (75%, 81%, 74%) — stronger than generic claim copy
Email-to-Trello Inbox flow is a genuinely novel feature clearly explained with concrete mechanism
Testimonials span very different industries and team contexts (nonprofit leadership, enterprise consulting, global retail/manufacturing) — broad applicability signaling
Free plan is front-and-center throughout without feeling desperate — trust-building rather than pressure-driven
Team vertical segmentation (marketing, engineering, design, startups, remote) efficiently qualifies diverse visitors
